name: Champagne Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the champagne, lemon juice, and powdered pectin in a heavy saucepan.
2. Cook over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil, stirring constantly.
3. Immediately add sugar to the boiling mixture.
4. Continue cooking, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches a full, rolling boil.
5. Remove from heat, skim off foam with a metal spoon, and pour into hot sterilized jars.
6. Seal the jars (using the hot water bath method) or refrigerate for up to 6 weeks.
Notes: A wonderful spread for tea breads, scones, or biscuits. It makes an excellent coating for white or pound cake, and can be used as a glaze on broiled fruits for a brunch treat. This jelly also serves well as a substitute for apple jelly in fruit tarts and other fruit desserts. For the best results, try using Domaine Ste-Michelle Champagne (about $6 per 750 ml bottle) which is both a great value and delicious to drink.
